extraction.one_line_summary: DEMOULIN Rudy attacked under 2km to go on uphill finish to Vence, holding off favorites after Wellens/Yates earlier bridge failed
tactical_patterns: WELLENS Tim bridged to YATES Simon on Col d'Eze, but combined effort failed with 5km remaining showing insufficient power advantage.
AG2R-La Mondiale disrupted peloton rhythm by forcing favorites to chase multiple moves rather than setting single sustained pace.
PORTE Richie abandoned while second GC with 5km remaining, suggesting illness/mechanical rather than dropped-on-climb weakness pattern.

rider_signals.DEMOULIN Rudy: positive: first WorldTour win launching inside 2km on uphill finish suggests similar steep ramps suit him better than classified climbs
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: first over final climb but couldn't sustain with Wellens, showing punch without diesel engine on extended efforts
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: bridging ability to reach leaders then still finishing second demonstrates rare diesel-punch combination for future breakaway viability
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: abandoning from second GC with 5km left indicates he was already compromised before final climb selection began
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: third place in reduced sprint after surviving final climb confirms emerging all-rounder status beyond pure mountain domestique role
